title: Smart hair dryer devices, systems, and methods
abstract: Attachment devices for applying and curing cosmetic compositions, such as hair dyes, to the hair of an individual, as well as hair dryer systems that include one or more such attachment devices. Methods of using the attachment devices and/or hair dryer systems for curing cosmetic compositions, and computational devices configured to perform all or part of the methods. Attachment devices can be comprised of an ultraviolet (UV) light attachment, an ion generator and aerosol applicator, a camera sensor attachment a proximity sensor attachment, and combinations thereof. Computational device-implemented variations of devices and methods.
claims: Claims CLAIMS The embodiments of the invention in which an exclusive property or privilege is claimed are defined as follows: 1. An attachment device configured for concomitant use with a hair dryer to apply and/or cure a cosmetic composition to a portion of a body of an individual, the attachment device comprising: an ultraviolet (UV) light configured to cure the cosmetic composition; an aerosol device that comprises an ion generator configured to ionize the cosmetic composition, and an applicator configured to transmit the cosmetic composition from the applicator; a camera sensor operably connected to circuitry, wherein the circuitry is configured to image, with the camera sensor, the portion of the body of the individual for a diagnosis and an application of the cosmetic composition based at least in part on the diagnosis; a proximity sensor operably connected to circuitry and configured to transmit data that corresponds to a distance between the attachment device and the portion of the body of the individual to the circuitry, wherein the circuitry is configured to control, based on data received from the proximity sensor, operation of the attachment device, the hair dryer, or both, to apply and cure the cosmetic composition; or any combination thereof. 2. The attachment device of claim 1, wherein the attachment device is an aftermarket device configured for use with the hair dryer and comprises a plurality of securements for securement of the attachment device to the hair dryer. 3. The attachment device of claim 1, wherein the attachment device is integral with the hair dryer as a unit. 4. The attachment device of any one of claims 1-2, wherein the plurality of securements is configured for securement of the attachment device to a securement rail of the hair dryer. 5. The attachment device of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the cosmetic composition is configured for application to hair of the individual and is UV photocurable. 6. The attachment device of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the cosmetic composition includes a hair dye that is curable with hot air of the hair dryer, UV light of the attachment device, or both. 7. The attachment device of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the applicator comprises a nozzle head configured to channel the cosmetic composition to and through a nozzle aperture of the applicator as an aerosolized cosmetic composition for spraying the aerosolized cosmetic composition onto the portion of the body of the individual. 8. The attachment device of any one of claims 1-7, further comprising a thermal sensor and circuitry configured to compute a hair dryer blower level, a hair dryer blower temperature, and/or an applicator spray level based at least in part on data captured by the thermal sensor of the hair dryer and/or attachment device. 9. The attachment device of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the circuitry is configured to: activate the applicator for application of the cosmetic composition to the portion of the body of the individual as an aerosolized cosmetic composition; and activate a blower of the hair dryer; wherein the hair dryer blows air based at least in part on the hair dryer blower level, the hair dryer blower temperature, and/or the applicator spray level. 10. The attachment device of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the diagnosis corresponds to a natural strand color, a strand density, a strand thickness, a strand length, one or more artificial strand colors, or any combination thereof. 11. The attachment device of any one of claims 1-10, wherein the circuitry is configured to compute a hair dryer blower level, a hair dryer blower temperature, and/or an applicator spray level based at least in part on the diagnosis. 12. The attachment device of any one of claims 1-11, wherein the cosmetic composition is selectable based at least in part on a natural strand color, a strand density, a strand thickness, a strand length, one or more artificial strand colors, or any combination thereof, of hair of the individual. 13. A hair dryer comprising a securement rail configured for securement of the attachment device of any one of claims 1-12 thereto. 14. A hair dryer system comprising the hair dryer of claim 13, optionally further comprising the attachment device of any one of claims 1-12. 15. A method for applying and curing a cosmetic composition with the hair dryer system of claim 14, the method comprising: applying, with the attachment device, the cosmetic composition to a surface; activating a blower of the hair dryer, such that the hair dryer blows air onto the cosmetic composition on the surface; and activating a UV light of the attachment device, such that UV light emitted from the attachment device irradiates the cosmetic composition on the surface; wherein the attachment device is attached to the hair dryer.
